LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LAZARD FRERES & CO. LLC DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO FIRST, FOURTH, SIXTH, AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND, THIRD, AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE WHETHER EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENT WRITTEN, ORAL OR IMPLIED BY CONDUCT, OR ATTACH A COPY OF THE WRITTEN AGREEMENT. TO SUPPORT ESTOPPEL BY CONDUCT CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE THAT DEFENDANT MADE A REPRESENTATION THAT PLAINTIFF WOULD RECEIVE A BONUS WHILE KNOWING OTHERWISE. PLAINTIFF HAS TEN DAYS TO FILE AMENDED PLEADING, ATTACHING CONTRACT TO THE PLEADING. ALSO, DEFENDANT MAY BRING A NEW DEMURRER AND OR MOTION TO STRIKE TO PLAINTIFF'S NEW PLEADING, AND RE-ARGUE THE OVERRULED CAUSES OF ACTION. DEFENDANT HAS 30 DAYS LEAVE TO FILE RESPONSE TO PLAINTIFF'S NEW PLEADING. COUNSEL MAY, ALSO INSERT LANGUAGE REGARDING DISCOVERY MOTION TO COMPEL. (COUNSEL WILL MEET AND CONFER REGARDING THE PROPOSED ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR 5759

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-10-2009 Continued To Oct-09-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-12-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT LAZARD FRERES & CO. LLC DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: Mark C. Thomas, Esq. of Brownsten Thomas LLP (415) 986-1338, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Michael J. Nader, Esq. of Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati (415) 947-2000, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows. Demurrer is overruled in part and sustained in part. Demurrer overruled as to first, fourth, sixth, and seventh causes of action. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the second, third, and fifth causes of action. Plaintiff to allege whether employment agreement written, oral or implied by conduct, or attach a copy of the written agreement. To support estoppel by conduct cause of action, plaintiff must allege that defendant made a representation that plaintiff would receive a bonus while knowing otherwise. Plaintiff has ten days to file amended pleading, attaching contract to the pleading. Also, Defendant may bring a new Demurrer and or Motion to Strike to Plaintiff's new pleading, and re-argue the overruled causes of action. Defendant has 30 days leave to file response to plaintiff's new pleading. Counsel may, also insert language regarding Discovery motion to compel. (Counsel will meet and confer regarding the Proposed Order.)(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
